title: Mean-centering a constant finite signal gives zero

source.citation: Lloyd N. Trefethen, Spectral Methods in MATLAB, SIAM, 2000, Ch. 3, pp. 17-28.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: On a nonempty finite index set, subtracting the finite mean from a constant signal gives the zero centered signal.
assumptions: s is a finite index set
the real cardinality of s is nonzero
informal.expected_conclusion: The centered signal value is zero at every index.

declarations.name: finiteDC
declarations.statement: def finiteDC {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) : Real := ∑ i ∈ s, u i

declarations.name: finiteMean
declarations.statement: def finiteMean {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) : Real := finiteDC s u / (s.card : Real)

declarations.name: centeredSignal
declarations.statement: def centeredSignal {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) (i : ι) : Real := u i - finiteMean s u

declarations.name: finiteMean_const
declarations.statement: theorem finiteMean_const {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (c : Real) (hcard : (s.card : Real) ≠ 0) : finiteMean s (fun _ => c) = c

declarations.name: centeredSignal_const_zero
declarations.statement: theorem centeredSignal_const_zero {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (c : Real) (i : ι) (hcard : (s.card : Real) ≠ 0) : centeredSignal s (fun _ => c) i = 0

tasks.input: Translate that mean-centering a constant finite signal gives zero into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem centeredSignal_const_zero {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (c : Real) (i : ι) (hcard : (s.card : Real) ≠ 0) : centeredSignal s (fun _ => c) i = 0

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding centeredSignal, rewriting the finite mean of a constant signal, and simplifying.
tasks.target: Unfold centeredSignal, rewrite finiteMean_const, and use ring.

tasks.input: Find the local lemma that computes the finite mean of a constant signal.
